commit.message:
feat(redesign): premium visual overhaul, number reconciliation, performance fix

Visual:
- Hero carousel: SVG chevron arrows (w-12 h-12), gradient CTA buttons
- Typography: eliminated all sub-10px text, minimum now text-xs (12px)
- Sub-pages: unique color-identity hero banners (cyan/mercado, green/tech, purple/pesquisa, red/seguranca)
- Panorama: larger KPIs, larger section headers, premium glass styling

Numbers reconciled:
- Countries: 972→37 (filter ≥20 jobs, exclude garbage)
- Insights: 104→46 (only publishable)
- Sources: 85→36 (real count)
- Removed fake 'Capital Rastreado US$ 1.4T' KPI (funding data is mis-sourced)
- Replaced with 'Tech Trends' KPI using real tech_trends data (38K signals)
- Fixed '~85% EUA' to '~54% EUA'
- Patents filtered: 37K mock-generated excluded

Methodology page:
- Replaced fake source list (CrunchBase, PitchBook, LinkedIn API) with real connected sources
- Added REAL/MOCK badges per KPI (10 real, 10 mock)
- Added honesty disclaimer about unconnected sources

Performance:
- Created materialized view mv_papers_vs_jobs (85s query→<100ms)
- Charts API now returns in <1s instead of timing out

Panorama charts:
- Replaced empty 'Investimento por Tipo' panel with 'Geopolítica ACLED' (929K events)

commit.message:
test(protocol): add biome registry ordering and cross-crate consistency tests

Add tests to verify:
- Biome registry entries are alphabetically sorted (matching vanilla)
- Plains biome is at index 40 (matching vanilla registries.json)
- All synchronized registries produce stable iteration order
- Cross-crate: biome IDs in oxidized-world match protocol registry order

These tests guard against the HashMap ordering regression fixed in the
previous commit and catch any future drift between world biome IDs and
protocol registry order.

commit.message:
fix(protocol): preserve registry entry order with IndexMap

The RegistryMap inner type was HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> which
has non-deterministic iteration order (Rust's RandomState). When
minecraft:dimension_type entries were sent in random order during
configuration, the hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 in the login packet
would randomly map to the wrong dimension type (e.g. the_nether with
min_y=0 and no skylight instead of overworld with min_y=-64).

This caused intermittent startup issues where:
- Chunks appeared to start from Y=0 instead of Y=-64
- Skybox rendering was wrong
- Blocks were not breakable

The fix:
1. Replace inner HashMap with IndexMap to preserve JSON insertion order
   (matching vanilla's registration order)
2. Add get_registry_entry_index() to look up entry indices by name
3. Replace hardcoded dimension_type_id=0 with dynamic lookup

BREAKING CHANGE: indexmap workspace dep now requires serde feature

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: Fix GitHub Pages deployment workflow

- Use official actions/deploy-pages instead of peaceiris
- Properly upload out/ directory
- Set correct permissions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"

refactor(server): config cleanup — remove Java leftovers & extract hardcoded values

Part A: Remove 3 Java-specific config fields with no Rust equivalent:
- is_jmx_monitoring_enabled (Java JMX monitoring API)
- is_native_transport_enabled (Netty epoll/kqueue toggle)
- is_sync_chunk_writes (Java threading model toggle)

Part B: Extract ~25 hardcoded constants into config structs:
- NetworkTimeoutsConfig: keepalive interval/timeout, login/configuration/write timeouts
- RateLimitConfig: max connections per window, window duration, cleanup interval
- Channel capacities: inbound (128) and outbound (512) channel buffer sizes
- EntityTrackingConfig: per-type tracking ranges (player/animal/monster/misc/projectile/default)
- WeatherConfig: rain and thunder delay/duration min/max values
- World tuning: chunk_cache_size (1024) and max_concurrent_chunk_generations (64)

All constants preserved as documented defaults. Validation ensures keepalive_timeout
>= keepalive_interval, weather min <= max, and positive cache/concurrency values.

Closes R5.18.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Dependabot security alerts #45, #47, #48: upgrade npm and Python build dependencies (#1065)

* Initial plan

* Upgrade mocha and add overrides to fix npm security vulnerabilities

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/d7dbd434-2d27-4a08-914d-d8410e3a63e4

* Address Dependabot alerts: upgrade setuptools and actions/checkout

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

commit.message:
feat(game): complete R5.17 entity selector completeness

Implement runtime enforcement for gamemode, distance, and type filters
in entity selector resolution. Previously only name/limit/sort were
applied; all other parsed filters were stored but ignored.

Changes:
- Add GameMode::from_name()/name()/ALL_NAMES for string conversion
- Add ServerHandle::get_player_game_mode() and get_player_position()
- Apply gamemode= filter (with ! negation) using player game mode data
- Apply distance= filter using Euclidean distance from command source
- Apply type= filter (player/minecraft:player vs other entity types)
- Add tab-completion for 20 filter keys inside @x[...] brackets
- Add value-level completion for sort= and gamemode= filter values
- Add 22 new tests (resolution, tab-completion, GameMode conversion)

commit.message:
feat(world,protocol): complete R5.16 safety hardening & cleanup

- Bound all network-facing NBT reads with default_quota() (2 MiB)
  instead of unlimited(), matching vanilla's network NBT limit
  (read_component_nbt + clientbound_registry_data decode)

- Update fluid/ticking counters in chunk sections:
  - set_block_state() now maintains fluid_count, ticking_block_count,
    and ticking_fluid_count via BlockStateId property lookups
  - recalculate_counts() recalculates all four counters
  - from_parts() calls recalculate_counts() for full accuracy
  - filled() computes correct counters for the fill block
  - Add PalettedContainer::for_each_value() for efficient iteration
  - 14 new unit tests for counter transitions

- Evaluate reqwest: decision to keep (documented in auth.rs module doc)
  Only 1 HTTP call, all features required, switching breaks public API

- Items 2/3/4 (rate limiter disconnect, player removal, mining_pos)
  were already implemented in prior phases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(server): replace NoopServerHandle with Weak<Self> self-ref

Replace the static NoopServerHandle with a Weak<ServerContext> self-
reference so send_command_tree_update uses the real ServerHandle
implementation. This ensures requires() predicates that check server
state during command tree serialization get live data.

- Add self_ref: OnceLock<Weak<Self>> field to ServerContext
- Add init_self_ref() method, called after Arc construction
- Remove NoopServerHandle struct and its ServerHandle impl

commit.message:
feat(server): per-player operator permissions via ops.json (R5.15)

Implement ops.json-based per-player permission system matching vanilla behavior:

- Add OpsStore (DashMap<Uuid, OpEntry>) with load/save for ops.json
- Extend ServerHandle trait with is_op, get_permission_level, op_player,
  deop_player, op_names, non_op_player_names methods
- Wire SharedOpsStore into ServerContext, loaded on startup
- Replace all 4 hardcoded op_permission_level sites with per-player lookup:
  - Command execution (play/mod.rs)
  - Command source creation (play/commands.rs, ×2)
  - EntityEventPacket on join (play/join.rs)
- Update is_spawn_protected: operator bypass, empty-ops disables protection
- Implement /op and /deop commands (replace stubs) with GameProfile argument
- Send permission level update (EntityEventPacket) + command tree resend on op/deop
- 12 unit tests for OpsStore, 2 new spawn protection tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(workspace): add benchmark & fuzz testing infrastructure (R5.14)

- Add criterion 0.5 as workspace dev-dependency with html_reports
- Create benchmark suites in 4 crates (38 benchmarks total):
  - oxidized-nbt: NBT read/write, SNBT format/parse, compound ops
  - oxidized-protocol: VarInt/VarLong encode/decode, packet framing
  - oxidized-world: block state lookup, tag membership
  - oxidized-game: physics properties, block categorization
- Set up cargo-fuzz targets in 3 crates (4 targets total):
  - oxidized-nbt: fuzz_nbt_read
  - oxidized-protocol: fuzz_varint, fuzz_packet_decode
  - oxidized-world: fuzz_paletted_container
- Extract ConnectionStateMachine from Connection for I/O-free testing
  with 13 unit tests covering all valid/invalid transitions
- Document benchmarks and fuzz testing in CONTRIBUTING.md
- Add fuzz artifact/corpus patterns to .gitignore

commit.message:
refactor(server,world,game): break down long functions & reduce nesting

R5.12 — decompose functions exceeding 200 LOC, reduce nesting depth,
and fix unsafe type casts.

handle_play_split (526→328 LOC): Extract keepalive handling, chat
command dispatch, swing/abilities/client-info packets, and player
cleanup into dedicated functions.

handle_use_item_on (297→248 LOC): Extract decrement_held_item and
place_companion_block helpers.

handle_player_action (235→190 LOC): Extract handle_stop_destroy for
the StopDestroyBlock match arm.

broadcast_player_join (212→46 LOC): Extract player info packet
building, entity broadcasting, and existing-player data collection
into 5 focused helpers.

dispatcher::parse (depth 8→5): Introduce ChildMatch enum and
try_match_child helper. Use match guards to eliminate nested ifs.

collect_child_suggestions (depth 9→6): Extract suggest_for_argument
with early-return inversion.

paletted_container.rs: Replace 3 unsafe i32→u32/usize casts from
VarInt network reads with checked try_from() conversions. Add
NegativeValue error variant. Add SAFETY comments to retained casts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Oxidized
repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust.

commit.message:
refactor(server): decompose oversized structs (R5.11)

Decompose 4 large structs into logical sub-structs:

- PrimaryLevelData (21 → 4 fields): SpawnPoint, WorldTime, WeatherState,
  WorldSettings
- Entity (19 → 12 fields): Vec3 pos/velocity, EntityRotation,
  EntityDimensions
- ServerPlayer (45 → 16 fields): PlayerMovement, CombatStats,
  PlayerExperience, SpawnInfo, ConnectionInfo, TeleportTracker,
  MiningState, RawPlayerNbt
- ServerContext (23 → 6 fields): WorldContext, NetworkContext,
  ServerSettings

All sub-structs are public with re-exports. All call sites updated.

commit.message:
refactor(world): compile-time item ID codegen (R5.7)

Replace runtime ItemRegistry (LazyLock + AHashMap + gzip decompression)
with compile-time generated static arrays, matching the existing block
codegen pattern.

Changes:
- build.rs: add generate_item_ids() producing item_ids_generated.rs
  with ITEM_NAMES, ITEM_MAX_STACK_SIZES, ITEM_MAX_DAMAGES, and
  ITEM_NAMES_SORTED (for binary search lookup)
- ItemRegistry: zero-sized struct wrapping static arrays (const fn new)
- item_ids.rs: replace LazyLock<ItemRegistry> with const REGISTRY
- Add snapshot test over all 1506 item names, full round-trip test,
  and spot-check tests with property verification

Zero startup latency — no decompression, no hashmap construction.
Generated file lives in $OUT_DIR/, never in source tree.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Oxidized
repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust.

refactor(world): replace hardcoded biome resolution with registry lookup (R5.6)

Create biome_registry module in oxidized-world with all 65 vanilla biomes
sorted alphabetically (matching protocol ID assignment). Provides O(log n)
binary search via biome_name_to_id() and O(1) biome_id_to_name().

- New: crates/oxidized-world/src/registry/biome_registry.rs
- Remove hardcoded 5-biome match in flat generator, use registry
- Remove local BIOME_NAMES from chunk_serializer, delegate to registry
- Replace linear .position() scan in chunk_loader with binary search
- 8 unit tests + 2 doc tests for the new biome registry

commit.message:
refactor(game): replace hardcoded physics properties with registry data (R5.5)

Delete PhysicsBlockProperties dense lookup table and PHYSICS_OVERRIDES array.
Physics properties (friction, speed_factor, jump_factor) are now read
directly from the compile-time block registry via BlockStateId methods,
which are already O(1) static array lookups.

Changes:
- Delete block_properties.rs (PhysicsBlockProperties, PHYSICS_OVERRIDES)
- Remove 7 redundant constants from constants.rs (ICE_FRICTION, etc.)
- Remove block_physics parameter from physics_tick(), block_speed_factor(),
  block_jump_factor()
- Use BlockStateId::friction/speed_factor/jump_factor directly in tick.rs
  and slow_blocks.rs
- Slime detection via BlockStateId::block_name() comparison
- Fix powder snow: speed_factor is 1.0 (vanilla property), not 0.9
  (that comes from makeStuckInBlock runtime behavior)
- Add 8 registry value verification tests
- Update memories.md with new physics pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(server): replace string-based block categorization with flags/tags (R5.4)

Replace 8 hardcoded string-matching functions in placement.rs with O(1)
BlockStateFlags checks and O(log n) BlockTags lookups:

- is_replaceable_block → BlockStateId::is_replaceable() flag
- is_interactable_block → BlockStateId::is_interactable() flag + beds tag
- is_wall_mountable → oxidized:wall_mountable tag
- is_player_direction_block → oxidized:player_direction tag
- is_sign_block → minecraft:all_signs tag
- is_door_block → minecraft:doors tag
- is_bed_block → minecraft:beds tag
- is_tall_plant → oxidized:tall_plants tag

Also:
- Add AIR_ITEM_NAME constant in item_stack.rs
- Add get_block_state_id() to ServerHandle trait
- Fix cmd_setblock keep-mode to use BlockStateId::is_air() (handles
  cave_air and void_air)

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

commit.message:
feat(world): implement block tag loading from vanilla data (R5.3)

Add compile-time block tag registry generated by build.rs from vanilla
tags.json and custom Oxidized tag files. Tags are stored as static
sorted arrays enabling O(log n) membership testing with zero startup
cost.

Vanilla tags: 248 block tags loaded from tags.json (produced by
bundle_tags.py), containing block type IDs for categories like
minecraft:doors, minecraft:beds, minecraft:replaceable, etc.

Custom Oxidized tags (4):
- oxidized:interactable (33 blocks) — crafting tables, furnaces, etc.
- oxidized:wall_mountable (64 blocks) — buttons, wall torches, etc.
- oxidized:player_direction (130 blocks) — beds, doors, stairs, etc.
- oxidized:tall_plants (8 blocks) — sunflower, tall_grass, etc.

Public API: BlockTags (zero-sized) with contains(), get(), tag_count(),
tag_names(). TagSet wraps sorted slice with O(log n) contains().

17 unit tests + 3 doc tests verify vanilla/custom tag membership,
positive/negative checks, sort invariants, and unknown tag handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(world): Enrich BlockStateEntry with physics properties (R5.2)

Add 10 new fields to BlockStateEntry: light_emission, light_opacity, hardness,
explosion_resistance, friction, speed_factor, jump_factor, map_color, and
push_reaction. All values are extracted at compile-time from vanilla data and
stored in fixed-point format for O(1) access.

BlockStateEntry grows from 4 bytes to 18 bytes (525 KB total for 29,873 states).

Changes:
- Expand BlockStateEntry struct with 10 new properties (repr(C) layout)
- Update build.rs to load all properties from block_properties.json.gz
- Implement 9 accessor methods on BlockStateId with proper conversions:
  - light_emission/light_opacity: direct u8 access
  - hardness/explosion_resistance: f64 from fixed-point ×100
  - friction/speed_factor/jump_factor: f64 from fixed-point ×10000
  - map_color/push_reaction: direct u8 access
- Fixed-point encoding handles extreme values via clamping (e.g. barrier)
- All 186 unit tests pass; spot-checked 8 well-known blocks

This eliminates the need for string-based physics property lookups and
hardcoded arrays in oxidized-game/src/physics/block_properties.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(world): enrich BlockStateFlags with data-driven block properties (R5.1)

Expand BlockStateFlags from u8 (3 flags) to u16 (12 flags) per ADR-012.
New flags: IS_SOLID, HAS_COLLISION, IS_OPAQUE, IS_REPLACEABLE,
HAS_BLOCK_ENTITY, TICKS_RANDOMLY, REQUIRES_TOOL, IS_FLAMMABLE,
IS_INTERACTABLE.

Add Python extraction script (tools/extract_block_properties.py) that
parses Blocks.java builder chains, BlockEntityType.java, and class
hierarchy to produce per-block property data for all 1168 vanilla blocks.

Update build.rs to read block_properties.json.gz and compute flags.
Add 9 convenience methods on BlockStateId for O(1) flag checks.
Add 10 verification tests covering flag correctness.

docs(phases): add Phase R5 — Data-Driven Dispatch & Code Deduplication

Plan 12 sub-tasks addressing four categories of tech debt found in
post-R4 audit:

- R5.1–R5.5: Enrich BlockStateFlags (u8→u16, 3→13 flags), expand
  BlockStateEntry with block properties, implement block tag loading,
  replace 114+ string-based block categorizations with flags/tags,
  replace hardcoded physics overrides with registry data
- R5.6: Replace hardcoded 5-biome match with registry lookup
- R5.7–R5.9: Extract packet codec helpers & roundtrip test macro,
  command registration helpers, standardize decoder error handling
- R5.10–R5.12: Decompose oversized structs (45-field ServerPlayer etc.),
  break down long functions (>200 LOC), replace magic numbers with
  named constants

Implements ADR-011 (tags) and ADR-012 (enriched block state) which were
accepted but had a large implementation gap. Data pipeline follows the
established pattern: extraction script → committed .json.gz → build.rs.

commit.message:
Clean typography: single sans-serif font, solid divider, allergies always shown

- Single font (system-ui sans-serif) throughout
- 3 sizes only: 0.75rem labels, 0.85rem message, 0.9rem values
- Weight used for hierarchy: 700 labels, 600 names, 400 values
- Solid vertical divider between left/star, inset from top/bottom
- Allergies field always visible (shows dash if empty)
- Dashed cut lines between cards, no outer border

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish stargram cards: dotted cut lines, cleaner typography, bigger star

- Dashed cut lines between cards (no outer border)
- Serif labels (Georgia) for a polished print feel
- Inline label: value layout with natural wrapping
- Allergies only shown when relevant (not 'no'/'none')
- Message in italic serif below To/From with subtle separator
- Vertically centered content in all columns
- Larger star (150px)
- Clean B&W print output, 4 per page

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(server): fix 6 block interaction and world persistence bugs

1. Seed persistence: save level.dat immediately after new-world
   initialization so seed and spawn_y survive crashes before the
   first autosave tick.

2. Bed head placement: read the facing property from the foot block
   state instead of recalculating with an incorrect .opposite() call.
   The head is now placed in the correct direction (matching vanilla
   BedBlock.setPlacedBy).

3. Block facing reversed: add is_player_direction_block() to identify
   blocks that face in the player look direction (beds, doors, stairs,
   fence gates, trapdoors, repeaters, comparators). Remove the incorrect
   piston/observer special case that omitted .opposite() — vanilla
   DirectionalBlock uses .getOpposite() for those too.

4. Player self-placement: add AABB intersection check that prevents
   placing blocks inside the player bounding box (0.6×1.8 standing,
   0.6×1.5 sneaking), preventing self-suffocation.

5. Block use vs placement priority: check if the clicked block is
   interactable (crafting table, chest, furnace, bed, etc.) and the
   player is not sneaking before attempting placement. When the block
   is interactable and the player is not sneaking, placement is
   suppressed (vanilla: state.useItemOn() takes precedence).

6. Spawn Y / void fall: same root fix as #1 — immediate level.dat
   save ensures spawn_y=-60 is persisted for flat worlds instead of
   defaulting to the legacy spawn_y=64 on reload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Oxidized
repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust.

commit.message:
Remove deprecated Datamart from item registry

Datamart has been deprecated by Microsoft. Removed from:
- item-type-registry.json (full item entry)
- script-config.json (taskflow node mapping)

commit.message:
Enrich aliases for all 45 registry items

Add ~90 new aliases across 42 items covering:
- Common abbreviations (lh, wh, dw, eh, nb, sjd, udf, env, pq, etc.)
- Industry terms (dataset, power query, data warehouse, rdl, etc.)
- Platform names (fabric lakehouse, spark notebook, kusto database, etc.)
- Alternate spellings (lake house, event stream, data mart, etc.)

repository.name: python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate
repository.description: Python monorepo boilerplate for serverless AWS applications, powered by uv, ruff, and AWS CDK.

commit.message:
fix(server): persist world seed and fix biome deserialization

- ServerHandle::seed() now returns the actual world seed from level_data
  instead of hardcoded 0
- New worlds initialize world_seed from config: parse as i64 for numeric
  seeds, hash string seeds, or generate random if empty
- Biome deserialization now looks up actual registry IDs from biome names
  in the alphabetically-sorted vanilla biome list instead of using
  sequential indices (0,1,2...), which caused plains (ID 40) to become
  badlands (ID 0) after save/load — producing wrong skybox and colors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Real-Time Dashboard and Map task_type: track -> visualize

Both are visualization items incorrectly tagged as track:
- Real-Time Dashboard (KQLDashboard): presents real-time data in dashboards
- Map: geospatial data visualization (now GA)

Also fixed track legend: removed stale AnomalyDetector reference
(AnomalyDetector is correctly tagged train, not track).
Updated visualize legend to include Real-Time Dashboard and Map.

Full audit confirms all 45 items are now correctly classified.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
